content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

May 18, 2016

Dennis R. La Salle
Manager
La Salle Solutions, LLC
900 F Street, Suite 128
San Diego, CA 92101

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-17-074

Dear Mr. La Salle:

This letter responds to your request for advice regarding Government Code Section 1090.¹
Please note that we do not advise on any other area of law, including Public Contract Code or
common law conflicts of interest. Additionally, our advice does not interpret local agency conflict
of interest policies. Local agencies can have rules that are stricter than Section 1090 or the Political
Reform Act. You state that in addition to requesting advice from us, you also contacted (and
provided these facts to) the city officials who will interpret the city policy. Additionally, we are not
a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we
provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate.

Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all
pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Diego County
District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a
written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that,
for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding
against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).)

QUESTION

Where LaSalle Solutions, LLC ("La Salle") has provided project management scheduling
support services to a prime contractor on the City of San Diego's Pure Water Project, does Section
1090 preclude La Salle from participating on a team competing for a Request for Proposals ("RFP")
for construction management services for the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure (pipelines and
facilities)?

___________________________

¹ Includes Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. All statutory references are to the Government
Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections
18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division
6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-17-074
Page No. 2

CONCLUSION

Section 1090 is not applicable to La Salle where its project management scheduling support
service was of a limited, technical nature, and there are no facts to support that La Salle was
engaged in the development of the construction management RFP, or had the potential to exert
considerable influence in the contracting decisions related to the development of the RFP.

FACTS

You are the manager of La Salle, a construction and project management support services
firm. La Salle is currently a sub-consultant of MWH Americas, Inc. ("MWH") on the Pure Water
San Diego Program for the City of San Diego ("City"). The Pure Water San Diego Program is a
phased, multi-year program to provide one-third of San Diego's water supply locally by 2035. The
Pure Water Program transforms the City's water system into a complete water cycle that maximizes
the use of water through recycling facilities located throughout the City. MWH Americas, Inc., is a
construction engineering company that provides services to municipalities in the areas of water and
wastewater treatment.

MWH Contract: In 2014, the City contracted with MWH for "As-needed engineering
technical consultant services" on this project. MWH is to provide the City with professional
engineering technical services, on an "as-needed," hourly fee basis. Under this contract, the City
may contract with more than one consultant for these professional services, due to its concerns that
MWH may not be able to provide the services on an as-needed basis. (City of San Diego, As-
Needed Agreement with MWH Americas, Inc., Revised 09-05-14, p.1, "Agreement.") The scope of
services is to be determined by the City on an as-needed basis and expressed in "task orders." (Id.,
p.2.) The Task Administrator is the Public Utilities Department ("PUD") and MWH is to perform
under the direction of the PUD's designated representative. (Id.) The City retains the right of prior
approval for any subcontractors hired to perform MWH's services. (Id., p. 9.)²

MWH's scope of services under the 2014 Agreement with the City included:

• Public education and Stakeholder engagement
• Permitting and Regulatory support
• Environmental Consultant coordination
• Design support
• Planning and Engineering support (with the possibility of extending beyond the 5-year
contract)
• Program administration and controls

² Additionally, the Agreement has a clause requiring any subcontractor to MWH amounting to "1% or more of
the value of [MWH's] Professional Services agreed to under this Agreement" to obtain prior written consent to
participate in services related in any way to the services covered by the Agreement: "Section 4.10.3 The Consultant and
its Subcontractors having subcontracts amounting to 1% or more of the value of the Professional Services agreed to
under this Agreement are precluded from participating in design services on behalf of the contractor, construction
management, and any other construction services related in any way to these Professional Services without the prior
written consent of the City." (Agreement, Article IV, Consultant's Obligations, Section 4.10.3, p. 13.)

File No. A-17-074
Page No. 3

(Agreement, Exhibit A-Scope of Services, pp. 1-14.)

To meet these services, MWH contracted with twenty-eight subcontractors, including
engineering firms, architects, land surveyors, multi-disciplinary firms (urban planning and
community involvement; transportation planning and traffic engineering), aerial mapping firms, and
La Salle, your support service firm.

Under its "Program and Administration Controls" duties, MWH is to:

I.8 Develop an overall Program Schedule in Primavera P6 or equivalent
software. The schedule shall incorporate the work breakdown structure
format and identify key milestones and timelines. Identify potential
conflicts or long lead-time items and find timely cost-effective
resolutions to conflicts as they arise, schedule recovery strategies, and
review schedules submitted by design consultants and design-builders.
On a monthly basis, prepare and update the master schedule for the entire
program based on actual progress. Perform Critical Path Method (CPM)
analysis as necessary.

(Agreement, Exhibit A-Scope of Services, p. 3.)

La Salle's Subcontractor Role: In its capacity as a "project management support service"
firm, La Salle provides MWH with a "scheduler," Ms. Krystal Merical. She works under the
direction of MWH's Project Manager.³ (June 2016 "Task Order to Master Services Contract" with
MWH.) Ms. Merical is trained in preparing schedules for design and construction projects using
the P6 program scheduling software. On the project, you state that Ms. Merical inputs the "line
items and duration of each line item, as provided to her by the Project Managers of each
project" into the scheduling software program and the schedule is prepared based on the data
received. You state that your firm's role is that of data input only, and that any decisions are made
by the individual project managers: "Our scheduler only prepares the schedule based on the data she
receives from the Project Managers. The decisions are made by the Project Manager."

The Task Order of June 2016⁴ sets forth the following duties for the scheduler:

Consultant Scheduler

Responsible for developing and maintaining the Program cost-loaded schedule,
including:

• Perform Monthly Project Schedule Updates, which include the following:

³ Under the Agreement, the City set forth a compensation schedule for an Engineer, Estimator, and Scheduler
from La Salle. (Agreement, Ex. C, "Compensation and Fee Schedule.") Per a subsequent phone conversation, you state
that La Salle only provided a scheduler.

⁴ The City documents submitted with your request for advice lists your firm's "potential participation" as
$300,000, for 1% of the prime contract.

File No. A-17-074
Page No. 4

○ Work with City Project Managers to update project schedules to
reflect actual progress and forecasted performance and identify
corrective actions to address schedule issues and to incorporate
these corrective actions into the project schedule as a revised
forecast
○ Manage and update Project Baseline Schedules in accordance with
the Program's Change Management Plan
○ Create a monthly key milestone status report for all projects
○ Run Monthly Project Schedule Update Reports that summarize
progress on each Pure Water project, as well as variances against
the Baseline Schedule and Last-Month Schedule Forecast; the
target audience for these reports is City Project Managers, City
Project Engineers and City and CONSULTANT [MWH]
Construction Managers

• Perform Monthly Program Schedule Updates, which include the
following:
○ Analyze overall Program schedule to identify impact of an
individual project delay or change in schedule assumptions to
inter-related projects
○ Run Monthly Program Schedule Updates Reports to summarize
progress on the overall Program, as well as variances against the
Baseline Schedule and Last-Month Schedule Forecast (the target
audience for these reports is the Program Leadership and possibly
some external stakeholders)

• Perform Reviews of third-party consultant Schedules, which include the
following:
○ Review the schedule submittals and related correspondence
provided by third-party consultants as part of the design process
and advise the City Project Managers, CONSULTANT [MWH]
Deputy Lead Project Manager and City Lead Project Manager
regarding the adequacy and accuracy of those submittals
○ Review changes proposed by third-party consultants and determine
their potential impacts on projects and program schedules
○ Prepare schedule status reports for the City Project Managers and
City Project Engineers

• Perform Reviews of Construction Contractor Schedules, which include the
following:
○ Review schedule submittals and related correspondence provid
by Construction Contractors and recommend acceptance/rejection
○ Review changes proposed by Construction Contractors and
determine their potential impacts on projects and Program
schedules
○ Review consistency between the various schedule submittals
required of Construction Contractors
○ Perform various schedule analyses to support negotiations with
Construction Contractors on change requests and claims

File No. A-17-074
Page No. 5

• Prepare schedule status reports for the City Project Managers and
City and CONSULTANT Construction Managers
• Perform Annual Work Plan Updates, which include the following:
o Provide input into the annual work plan providing summary reports
on project schedule performance for past year and proposed project
schedule summaries for forthcoming year
• Provide training to City staff in the use of control tools and processes

Reports To:
Project Controls Manager

Although the Task Order contemplated a more expansive role, you state that the scheduler
has not performed reviews of third party consultant schedules or reviews of contractor construction
schedules. Additionally, you note that when schedulers review construction base line schedules
"they only make technical analysis and provide comments/recommendations and status reports for
the City of San Diego Project Managers. The City of SD PM's are the decision makers." Your
scheduler is also performing Project Controls administrative duties, which involves inputting
reports to the City's Prism software program.

The period for the Task Order was originally July 2016 to December 31, 2016. A Change
Order extended the period of performance to June 30, 2018. Recently MWH announced it will be
reducing its role as Project Manager, and the City will take over some project management tasks.
Your firm will continue to provide scheduling support services under the current task order until
June 30, 2018.

The Proposed Construction Management Contract: The Pure Water Project has three
phases: project management, detail design, and construction management. On January 28, 2017
MWH sent the following email information to twenty-four subcontractors:

All
Your firm has been involved in at least one Pure Water project since the start of
the program. If your firm supported the predesign of a specific project, you are
not eligible to be on a team competing for the detailed design of those facilities
for which our team has prepared the predesign documents. You may be contacted
by firms hoping to be selected for this work who are seeking information about
the project. At the city's request, I'm contacting your today to ask you not to
discuss the specifics of projects on which you had a role.

You state that your firm does not provide "design services," and you believe your firm is eligible to
participate in future construction management services.

The City of San Diego is currently preparing a RFP for the "Construction Management
services for the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure, (pipelines and facilities)" for which your firm
wishes to compete in its capacity as a "construction management support" firm. You seek advice as

File No. A-17-074
Page No. 6

to whether Section 1090's prohibition on a public officer making a contract in which he or she is
financially interested precludes you from doing so.⁵

For the upcoming RFP, La Salle wishes to provide construction inspectors for the pipeline
work. You state that your firm has not participated in any part of the development of plans or
specifications related to this RFP in performing its project management scheduling work on behalf
of MWH. The pipeline construction management contract will have its own, separate scheduler.
You state that it is "not possible" for the La Salle firm to have an influence or make decisions
related to the scheduling of pipeline inspections.

ANALYSIS

Section 1090

Section 1090 bars public officials, including city officers or employees, from being
personally financially interested in the contracts they form in their official capacities. (Davis v.
Fresno Unified School District (2015) 237 Cal. App. 4th 261, 298; Lexin v. Superior Court (2010)
47 Cal.4th 1050, 1072.) Section 1090's goals include eliminating temptation, avoiding the
appearance of impropriety, and assuring the public of the official's undivided and uncompromised
allegiance. (Thomson v. Call (1985) 38 Cal.3d 633, 648.) Under Section 1090, "the prohibited act is
the making of a contract in which the official has a financial interest." (People v. Honig (1996) 48
Cal.App.4th 289, 333.) A contract that violates Section 1090 is void. (Id. at p. 646.)

The term "public officer" is interpreted broadly under Section 1090 and includes
independent contractors who perform a public function and have the potential to exert considerable
influence over the contracting decisions of a public agency." (See Hub City Solid Waste Services,
Inc. v. City of Compton (2010) 186 Cal.App.4ᵗʰ 1114, 1124-1125; citing California Housing
Finance Agency v. Hanover/California Management & Accounting Center, Inc. (2007) 148
Cal.App.4ᵗʰ 682, 690-693; see also Davis, supra at pp.300-301.) The purpose behind this inclusive
definition is to ensure that independent contractors who are essentially performing a public
function, though temporarily, provide the same "fealty expected from permanent officers and
employees." (46 Ops.Cal.Atty.Gen 74 (1965).)

In the recent matter of Davis, supra, the court concluded that a contractor: (1) was an
"employee" for purposes of Section 1090 and (2) participated in making the contracts, where it was
alleged that the contractor served as a professional consultant to the public entity and had a hand in
designing and developing the plans and specifications for the school building project that the
contractor later entered into contract to build. (Davis, supra, at 301.)

We have advised that sub-consultants who play a limited technical role and are removed
from influencing the contracting decisions of a public agency may not be subject to Section 1090.

⁵ You requested this information from the City's Attorney office, but you were advised in a March 2, 2017
letter that the City Attorney can only advise elected officials, officers, and employees. The City Attorney referred you
to PUD, our office and the City Ethics Commission. You state that the City Ethics Commission informed you that it
does not respond to requests for legal opinions from private entities. You recently spoke with PUD officials, Halla
Razak and James Nagelvoort, who indicated that they "did not see a conflict" and recommended you obtain advice from
our office.

File No. A-17-074
Page No. 7

(Chadwick Advice Letter, No. A-15-147, p.12, see also Green Advice Letter No. A-16-084.) In
Chadwick, supra, while the primary consultants and a "highly involved sub-consultant" were found
to be subject to Section 1090, the sub-consultants who provided technical input, reports, and similar
information in a support role to the consultants, were not subject to Section 1090. These sub-
consultants were sufficiently removed from directly advising the city staff, and did not exert
considerable influence over the contracting decisions. (Chadwick 15, supra.) In Green, supra, an
audio-visual firm that provided technical expertise in drafting an equipment list and system flow-
chart to meet the system requested and stated needs of the local government was not deemed to
have exerted considerable influence over the local government's decision-making process; and
therefore, was not subject to Section 1090. (Green, supra, p. 3.)

Applying these principles to the facts in this matter, your firm is not acting as a "public
officer" in its capacity as a sub-consultant to MWH for the Pure Water San Diego Program. La
Salle provides support services of a technical nature to MWH and the City. La Salle's role is
removed from that of influencing contracting decisions. Additionally, La Salle has not been
involved with MWH or the City in the development of the upcoming RFP for construction
management services on the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure. Therefore, Section 1090 is not
applicable to La Salle under these facts.

Please note that this analysis pertains to only to Section 1090. The City or the Public
Utilities Department may have stricter contracting rules over and above Section 1090, and these
rules are beyond our jurisdiction.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Hyla P. Wagner
General Counsel

By: L. Karen Harrison
Counsel, Legal Division

LKH:jgl

for construction management services for the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure (pipelines and 
facilities)?

sections.conclusion:
Section l 090 is not applicable to La Salle where its project management scheduling support 
service was of a limited, technical nature, and there are no facts to support that La Salle was 
engaged in the development of the construction management RFP, or had the potential to exert 
considerable influence in the contracting decisions related to the development of the RFP.

chart to meet the system requested and stated needs of the local government was not deemed to 
have exerted considerable influence over the local government's decision-making process; and 
therefore, was not subject to Section 1090. (Green, supra, p. 3.) 
Applying these principles to the facts in this matter, your firm is not acting as a "public 
officer" in its capacity as a sub-consultant to MWH for the Pure Water San Diego Program. La 
Salle provides support services of a technical nature to MWH and the City. La Salle's role is 
removed from that of influencing contracting decisions. Additionally, La Salle has not been 
involved with MWH or the City in the development of the upcoming RFP for construction 
management services on the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure. Therefore, Section 1090 is not 
applicable to La Salle under these facts. 
Please note that this analysis pertains to only to Section 1090. The City or the Public 
Utilities Department may have stricter contracting rules over and above Section 1090, and these 
rules are beyond our jurisdiction.

QUESTION: Where LaSalle Solutions, LLC ("La Salle") has provided project management scheduling 
support services to a prime contractor on the City of San Diego's Pure Water Project, does Section 
I 090 preclude La Salle from participating on a team competing for a Request for Proposals ("RFP") 
for construction management services for the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure (pipelines and 
facilities)? 
1 Includes Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5.

CONCLUSION: Section l 090 is not applicable to La Salle where its project management scheduling support 
service was of a limited, technical nature, and there are no facts to support that La Salle was 
engaged in the development of the construction management RFP, or had the potential to exert 
considerable influence in the contracting decisions related to the development of the RFP.

La Salle Manager La Salle Solutions, LLC 900 F Street, Suite 128 San Diego, CA 92101 Re: Your Request for Advice Our File No. A-17-074 Dear Mr. La Salle: This letter responds to your request for advice regarding Government Code Section 1090.¹ Please note that we do not advise on any other area of law, including Public Contract Code or common law conflicts of interest. Additionally, our advice does not interpret local agency conflict of interest policies. Local agencies can have rules that are stricter than Section 1090 or the Political Reform Act. You state that in addition to requesting advice from us, you also contacted (and provided these facts to) the city officials who will interpret the city policy. Additionally, we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71), and any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. Regarding our advice on Section 1090, we are required to forward your request and all pertinent facts relating to the request to the Attorney General's Office and the San Diego County District Attorney's Office, which we have done. (Section 1097.1(c)(3).) We did not receive a written response from either entity. (Section 1097.1(c)(4).) We are also required to advise you that, for purposes of Section 1090, the following advice "is not admissible in a criminal proceeding against any individual other than the requestor." (See Section 1097.1(c)(5).) QUESTION Where LaSalle Solutions, LLC ("La Salle") has provided project management scheduling support services to a prime contractor on the City of San Diego's Pure Water Project, does Section 1090 preclude La Salle from participating on a team competing for a Request for Proposals ("RFP") for construction management services for the Pure Water Phase 1 Infrastructure (pipelines and facilities)? ___________________________ ¹ Includes Government Code Sections 1090 through 1097.5. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.
